extraction.one_line_summary: KAISER Jonathan won from the breakaway on Etna, holding off a reduced peloton while YATES Simon lost contact 8km from the summit, severely damaging his GC.
tactical_patterns: Mitchelton-Scott worked early for Yates despite 150km remaining, revealing overconfidence that backfired when he cracked 8km out.
Experienced riders Nibali/Fuglsang/Kwiato formed defensive group finishing 55s down, signaling conservative first-summit GC tactics over attacking.
Almeida gained maglia rosa by racing rather than waiting for team leader Thomas's crash damage control.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: lost contact 8km from summit after team committed to early pace-setting, suggesting form issues or catastrophic tactical miscalculation
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: crashed in neutral zone then dropped 20km before summit, GC ambitions already compromised by Stage 3
rider_signals.KAISER Jonathan: positive: first win outside Ecuador came at Grand Tour level from breakaway on summit finish, suggests underrated climbing
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: opportunistically took maglia rosa while designated leader Thomas crashed, revealing team hierarchy fluidity
